title: Cal State LA History Quiz
subtitle: Founding, milestones, and programs
search_scope: CSULA California State University Los Angeles history founding campus programs
primer: California State University, Los Angeles (Cal State LA) was founded in 1947 as Los Angeles State College. It has grown into a diverse, research-oriented university with a rich history, including pioneering programs in Chicano Studies and Latin American Studies. The campus sits on historic land and has played a key role in the educational and cultural development of Los Angeles.

trivia_questions.question: In what year was Cal State LA founded?
trivia_questions.explanation: Cal State LA was founded in 1947 as Los Angeles State College through an act of the California legislature.

curated_sources.title: California State University, Los Angeles — Wikipedia
curated_sources.description: Overview of CSULA's founding and history.

trivia_questions.question: What was the original name of Cal State LA when it opened in 1947?
choices: California State College at Los Angeles
trivia_questions.explanation: The university was originally called Los Angeles State College before undergoing several name changes as it expanded.

curated_sources.title: Cal State LA History — Special Collections
curated_sources.description: History and evolution of Cal State LA.

trivia_questions.question: Which groundbreaking program was first established at Cal State LA in 1968?
trivia_questions.explanation: Cal State LA established the nation’s first Chicano Studies program in 1968, reflecting its commitment to cultural and social education.

curated_sources.title: Cal State LA History — Special Collections
curated_sources.description: Details on pioneering programs and historical milestones.

trivia_questions.question: What is the historical significance of the land where Cal State LA sits?
choices: It was part of Rancho Rosa Castilla, a historic Spanish/Mexican land grant
It was the site of Los Angeles’ first university
It was previously a gold mining site
It was the original site of the LA City Hall
trivia_questions.correct_answer: It was part of Rancho Rosa Castilla, a historic Spanish/Mexican land grant
trivia_questions.explanation: Cal State LA’s campus is located on land that was once part of Rancho Rosa Castilla, highlighting its historical roots in Southern California.
